Like [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|you]] happened... like [[May Parker (Earth-616)|she]] happened.|[[Peter Parker (Earth-616) |Spider-Man to Miles]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} '''Peter Benjamin "''B."'' Parker''' is a former student at [[Midtown High School]] and later at [https://comicvine.gamespot.com/empire-state-university/4020-43783/ Empire State University], being once an ordinary teenager, the young Peter's life was changed forever once he was bitten by a little [[radioactive spider|spider]] infused with radiation during an exposition about radioactivity which in result make him gained various superhuman abilities related to the arachnids. Initially using his newfound powers to win fame and money, following the death of his uncle, [[Benjamin Parker (Earth-616)|Ben Parker]], by a burglar he passed by. Peter learned through those events that with ''"With Great Power There Must Also Comes Great Responsibility"'' as he began to fight crime across [[Earth-616|New York City]] as a way to honor his Uncle by taking on the identity of the masked superhero known as '''Spider-Man'''. During his time as Spider-Man, Peter faced [[:Category:Villains|several villains]] who began to terrorize the city, such as the Chameleon, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vulture_(Marvel_Comics) Vulture], [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandman_(Marvel_Comics) Electro], [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandman_(Marvel_Comics) Sandman], [[Otto Octavius (Earth-616)|Doctor Octopus]], [[Quentin Beck (Earth-616)|Mysterio]] and others all while at the same time Peter began working as a photographer at ''[[Daily Bugle (Earth-616)|The Daily Bugle]]''. Eventually, Parker gained the affections of classmate [[Liz Allan]], as he began to develop feelings for his co-worker [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betty_Brant Betty Brant]. His superhero persona "Spider-Man" saved the city multiple times despite being controversial among the citizens and the law due to his status as a vigilante. After graduating high school, Parker began attending to the Empire State University while concurrently he began working side by side with the police captain [[George Stacy (Earth-616)|George Stacy]] despite his tense relationship with the law. During this time, Peter also met and became infatuated with the captain's daughter and fellow classmate, Gwen, and the growing attraction that had been building between them soon became a stable relationship, despite Peter maintaining his secret from her. However, sometime later, Captain Stacy was tragically killed during a fight between Spider-Man and one of his arch-nemeses, profoundly breaking Peter's heart. Things wouldn't get any better since during a fight against one of his villains Gwen was killed, since Spider-Man was unable to save her from falling to her death, leaving Peter devastated. After years of saving the city, Parker eventually coped with her death as he eventually entered a romantic relationship with the beautiful [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-616)|Mary Jane Watson]] and sometime later he would end up of getting married with the latter. As Parker quit working at ''[[Daily Bugle|The DB]]'' years after his marriage Spider-Man bonded with an [[Venom (Earth-616)|unknown substance]], which improved his abilities but made him a more violent and embittered vigilante. Eventually, after discovering the true nature of the [[Symbiotes|symbiote]], he got rid of it, but it bonded to a vengeful [[Edward Brock (Earth-616)|Eddie Brock]] which both of them forming [[Venom]]. After defeating the villain, Parker came into conflict with his best friend Harry, who was continuing his [[Norman Osborn (Earth-616)|father]]'s legacy by becoming the Green Goblin. During the fight Peter and Harry then reconciled their friendship, before Peter and MJ tearfully witnessed Harry's death. However, those events as well as the lose of more persons dying on Peter's life started to cause a toll on his body, both as vigilante and superhero as well as other aspects of his life, despite these newfound challenges, Peter continued to save the city as Spider-Man since he kept getting back up. Unfortunately, his life suddenly changed once his Aunt, [[Maybelle Parker (Earth-616)|May Parker]], ended up dying since Spider-Man couldn't save her in time. Guilt-ridden since those events Parker, under the belief that he and his alter-ego was dangerous to those around him, would decide to divorce his wife as well as isolate himself from everyone, including his friends and allies, in order to keep them safe from his vigilante persona. He then resumed his duties undisturbed as a now completely solitary Spider-Man. However as the years began to pass, his unhealthy ways to "protect" the people he loved eventually resulted on him becoming a rather worn-out version of the [[Spider-People|hero]] [[Peter Parker|he was before]]. Some time later, in [[2010s/Earth-616|2018]], Peter B. Parker was displaced to [[Earth-1610|another universe]] as a result of that world's [[Wilson Fisk (Earth-1610)|Kingpin]]'s [[Super-Collider|super-collider]]. Upon arriving there, Parker met [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]: a teenager with abilities similar to his own, for which he was forced to take him under his wing as a way to help Miles control his powers so that he could replace his [[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|deceased counterpart]] as the new Spider-Man of that reality as well as send him back to his own. However, after a [[Infiltration at Alchemax|failed]] [[Fight at Alchemax|attempt]] to infiltrate [[Alchemax]], the dynamic duo would later meet an [[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|alternate version]] of [[Gwen Stacy]], who turned out to be [[Earth-65|another dimension]]'s Spider-Woman. Sometime later after those events, the trio would arrive at that universe's [[Maybelle Parker (Earth-1610)|Aunt May's]] [[Aunt May's House|House]], where they would meet [[Peter Parker (Earth-90214)|another]] [[Peni Parker|three]] [[Peter Porker|displaced]] Spider-People and manage to form a team in order to return to their respective universes. Peter nicknamed the new group as the [[Spider-Gang]]. Unfortunately Peter and the group noticed how Miles wasn't prepared to become the new protector of his city, so Parker decided to sacrifice himself to destroy the machine and send everyone back to their respective realities. However at the end, Miles Morales victorious emerged as the new Spider-Man managing with the help of Parker to defeat Kingpin and his goons and send most of the displaced Spider-People back [[Earth-90214|to]] [[Earth-14512|their]] [[Earth-8311|universes]]. A little reluctantly at first, Peter would accept to return to his home reality after noticing that Miles was ready to take up his mantle in that Earth. After those events, Peter Parker started to gain a new positive outlook, being inspired by Miles Morales to try to make his life stable for once, as he began to missed the latter since he sees him as his pupil and friend. Over the next [[2020s/Earth-616#2023|five years]], following the [[Collider Crisis]], Peter not only finally mend his relationship with Mary Jane but he also began to recover his same agility as well as the happiness he had lose years ago. In the same period of time Peter would fathering a daughter named [[May Parker (Earth-616)|Mayday Parker]], who was named after Peter's deceased aunt. Managing to gain a great affection for his daughter, Peter would continue to defend his city as Spider-Man sometimes even taking his daughter to his missions. Sometime after becoming a "Spider-Dad", Peter would meet [[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Miguel O'Hara]], being recruited by him to the [[Spider-Society]], becoming one of its founding members. Peter alongside Miguel and the rest of team would faced many threats facing many multiversal-displaced villains and sending them to their respectives universes to avoid the space-time continuum collapse. As he was saving the multiverse, Parker would also try to balance his superhero life, with his responsibilities as both a father and a member of the Spider-Society. However things would drastically change once he along the rest of the Society wasn't able to prevent the destruction of a whole universe, which led to Miguel become more angry and guilt driven, following those events Spider-Man and the rest of the Spider-Society would found out about the [[Canon Events]] for which them make an wow to avoid the destruction of more universes by making sure the Canon Events gets full fill as they were forbidden to visiting the variant of Miles from 1610, including Peter. Sometime later after those events Peter get reunited with Gwen Stacy as well as Miles Morales, surprised of seeing his friend and thinking that he could finally form part of the team. However, the wholesome reunion would take an unexpected turn once Miguel explain to Miles about the Canon Events which results on Morales discovering the independent death of his father. After Miles decide to go back to his universe to save [[Jefferson Morales (Earth-1610)|his dad]], which result on [[Hunt for Miles Morales|a fight]] at [[Earth-928|Nueva York]], there Peter became the only one there who put himself on Miles' side even tough the latter will think otherwise following a misunderstanding that would make him leave the Spider-Society just to at then end be recruited along with his daughter by Gwen Stacy to be part of a new incarnation of the Spider-Gang as a way to found Miles who was displaced to [[Earth-42|another dimension]]. ==Biography== ===Early Life=== ====Growing Up in New York==== {{Biography Quote|You were such a nerd in [[Midtown High School|high school.]]"
"I watched sports, ok?|Peter Parker and [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-616)|Mary Jane]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Parker Residence (616).webp|thumb|right|250px||Peter's home]] Peter Benjamin Parker was born in Queens, New York City, to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_and_Mary_Parker Richard] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richard_and_Mary_Parker Mary Parker] in the year [[Before 21st Century/Earth-616#1980|1980]]. His parents became absent from his life during his childhood, leaving the young Parker to be raised by his loving aunt [[May Parker (Earth-616)|May Parker]] and uncle [[Ben Parker (Earth-616)|Ben Parker]]. As Peter grew up in Queens, he would develop a close relationship with both his Aunt and Uncle (due to them being the people who raised him), to the point that he begins to see them as if they were his parents. Peter - along with his Uncle - would watch some sports games, Ben having introduced him to the world of baseball, where he gained a kind of fanaticism for the New York Mets. As Peter was growing up, he gained a love for photography, beginning to use an old camera as a way to take photos in his free time.''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]''[https://variety.com/2015/film/news/sony-hack-peter-parker-spider-man-white-straight-1201524150/ Spider-Man Needs to Be White and Straight, Say Leaked Sony Emails] | Variety[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse|''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'']] ====Becoming Spider-Man==== {{Biography Quote|My name is Peter B. Parker. I was bitten by a radioactive spider. And for the last twenty-two years, I thought I was the one and only Spider-Man.|Spider-Man|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Peter becomes Spider-Man.png|thumb|right|250px|Peter gets bitten by the [[Peter Parker (Earth-90214)|little spider]] that was infused with radiation]] In his teens, Peter B. Parker attended [[Midtown High School]], where he quickly became a fan of anything related to the sciences, thus managing to be considered a nerd by most of his classmates; this was the reason he never had many friends and became the target of bullying, mainly perpetrated by [[Flash Thompson (Earth-616)|Flash Thompson]], as well as being ignored by popular girls like [[Liz Allan]]. Even though he is considered as a nerd by the girls and most of the kids of his school, Peter still watches sports and games in his free time, yet he always prefers the sciences over sports. In [[Before 21st Century/Earth-616#1996|1996]], at the age of fifteen, Peter Parker attended a science demonstration about radiation where he was bitten by a [[Radioactive Spider|little spider]] that was irradiated by the demonstration's particle accelerator. This event would manage to alter his physiology, which granted him several superhuman powers and abilities, including strength and speed proportional to that of a spider and an uncanny ability to adhere to walls. In addition of the capabilities of a spider, the spider bite also cured his myopia, which result in him discarding his glasses, as well as also managed to enhanced his muscle tissue and his perception managing to alter his weak and skinny body into something more muscular and athletic. [[File:Dead of Ben Parker.png|thumb|left|250px|Peter after losing his [[Benjamin Parker (Earth-616)|Uncle Ben]]]] Sometime after getting his powers, Peter fashioned a [[Spider-Man Suit|rudimentary costume]] for himself as he began using his powers to gain fame and money; as he began to enter and win a wrestling competition and later, as a celebrity at TV. Some time later, after those events his uncle, [[Ben Parker]], told him that, "with great power, there must also come great responsibility." At some point after this, during his days as a celebrity, Spider-Man allowed a mugger to run past him without using his abilities to stop the mugger, deciding it was "not [his] problem." This action led to Ben getting shot, who ended up dying in his arms. Angry by the events, Spider-Man began to hunt the killer down however just as he cornered the burglar at a warehouse, he would discover that he was the same thief he had allowed to escape minutes earlier. Now filled with remorse knowing that his inaction led to this tragedy, Peter realized the meaning behind his Uncle Ben's words, that "with great power comes great responsibility". Now resolved to use his abilities to help those in need, as a way to honored his deceased Uncle, Peter started to develop his own [[Synthetic Webbing|synthetic web fluid]] and a pair of special wrist-mounted [[web-shooters]] to battle against the criminals of New York City, ending up becoming the costumed superhero known as Spider-Man. ====Early Years as a Superhero==== {{Biography Quote|'Or everyone's gonna die.' That is what they always say. But there's always a little bit of time before everybody dies and that's when I do my best work.|Peter Parker to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Peter B. Parker 01.webp|thumb|right|250px|Spider-Man making his debut as a hero]] As New York's newest hero, he helped to stop petty crimes across the city and gained a reputation for either being a menacing vigilante or a saviour to the people. As he was defending the city from street-level crimes, Peter began to work as a freelance photographer at [[Daily Bugle (Earth-616)|''The'' ''Daily Bugle'']], as a way to to take photos of himself as Spider-Man in action to help his aunt pay the bills following Ben's passing, where he met [[Robbie Robertson]] and his boss [[J. Jonah Jameson (Earth-616)|J. Jonah Jameson]], with whom he formed a love-hate relationship. However, while the job brought him money, his pictures were often used by his boss to incriminated his alter ego to the point he ironically help Jonah in his smear campaign against the wall-crawler. As Spider-Man continued to protect the city despite those inconveniences, he would end coming into conflict with some criminals with special abilities similar to his, with the first one he ever face being Vulture, managing to have fight in the air against the winged criminal until he later defeated him after that Peter began to face other villains as Spider-Man to the point one of their more dangerous end up being [[Otto Octavius (Earth-616)|Doctor Octopus]] who came into conflict with Spider-Man until the latter finally managed to defeat him.[[File:Peter B. in his early years.webp|thumb|left|250px|Peter during his [[Before 21st Century/Earth-616#1996|early]] [[2000s/Earth-616|years]] as Spider-Man]] Sometime after this, Spider-Man fight against the reptilian monster known as the Lizard, who everyone tough it was a myth, Spider-Man eventually discover than the monster was no other than Curtis Connors, who became a monster after using its own serum to be able to get back his lose limb. After a fight in the sewers Peter managed to made a cure for the villain managing to finally return him into his human form. Sometime after those events Peter began to face more supervillains as Spider-Man for years, such as the Enforcers, Sandman, Electro, Mysterio, Chameleon, Kraven the Hunter along with many others more that started to threaten New York City. As Spider-Man kept saving the city and defeating many considerable foes all while at the same time as he was working for the The Bugle as a civilian while concurrently balancing his responsibilities as a high school student, Peter would still kept the same routine of ''"''wake up, go to school and save the world''"'' for the years to come with Parker sometimes defeating some of his old villains during this period of time as he continued to work for the Daily Bugle as a way to help her aunt pay the bills. However, once Peter enter his senior year, when six of his villains united as a way to bringing down, Spider-Man used all his strength to defeated the newly formed Sinister Six and avoid them to finally managing to controlled New York once and for all. Sometime after those event, Peter finally graduated from Midtown High School in [[Before 21st Century/Earth-616#1998|1998]]. Following his graduation, his former bully Flash as well as his former crush Liz would end up with Peter in good terms with them ending finally becoming close friends after graduating high school. However, thanks to his controversial reputation as a vigilante Spider-Man would also get into many conflicts with the law, specially the [[Wikipedia:New_York_City_Police_Department|NYPD]], since they do not trust Spider-Man or his actions, despite that, he gained the trust of the police Captain [[George Stacy (Earth-616)|George Stacy]], who became few of the police officers that trust Spider-Man as well as his desire to do the right thing. At the same time, Peter began to attend to [https://comicvine.gamespot.com/empire-state-university/4020-43783/ Empire State University] along his former bully-now-friend Flash, where he gets introduced to Harry Osborn, where he quickly befriended him as well as George's daughter Gwen Stacy, which he began to develop romantic feelings for. Sometime alter after those events, Peter would forcefully meet his [[Anna Watson|Aunt May's friend]]'s niece [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-616)|Mary Jane Watson]], which he would end becoming friend with the latter after a failed blind date planed by their aunts. As Peter continued to save New York City from any threat, Spider-Man would fought against many other supervillains, such as [[MacDonald Gargan (Earth-616)|Scorpion]], [[Lonnie Lincoln (Earth-616)|Tombstone]], [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shocker_(character) Shocker], [[Aleksei Sytsevich (Earth-616)|Rhino]], [[Joseph (Earth-616)|Hammerhead]], [[Wilson Fisk (Earth-616)|Kingpin]] and many others, however his most powerful villain to date end up being [[Norman Osborn (Earth-616)|The Green Goblin]], who turned out to be the father of Harry Osborn, having a bloody battle with the villain, Spider-Man finally managed to defeat him after much difficulty getting him behind bars where he belongs. After those events, Norman would become one of Spider-Man's most recurring villains, as well as Doctor Octopus, with him facing those villains many times, defeating them all equally as he bean to enter on a romantic relationship with Gwen, managing to have really healthy and stable relationship despite Peter deciding to keep his identity as a secret to her which result on he sometimes missing a few of their dates and some issues where he used his job at the Bugle as an excuse for all his flees. [[File:The Dead Of George Stacy.webp|thumb|right|250px|Parker mourning the death of [[George Stacy (Earth-616)|George Stacy]]]] Sometime later after those events, Spider-Man would once again with the Captain Stacy to stop Doctor Octopus who broke free from prison using a new version of his metallic arms, entering in a conflict with the supervillain, Spider-Man would battle him on a rooftop, however sadly those event leads to the dead of Captain Stacy who died as he was saving a child from falling debris, something which was witnessed by Spider-Man who, after managing to defeat Doc Ock, Peter would take him out of those falling debris as he held him in his arms. When Captain Stacy was dying, Peter is forced to promise him to take care of Gwen in his absence as well as revealing that he knew Spider-Man was Peter all along. After hearing this Peter would accept as he watch him die thing hat profoundly break his heart. A sad Peter later attends Captain Stacy's funeral, with both him and Gwen being broken by the demise of George Stacy. Even though that traumatic event still haunts him to this day, at the same time that event also help him to become a more competent Spider-Man, as he wanted to keep his promised of whatever he go to take cared of his girlfriend, either as Spider-Man or Peter Parker. However, sometime later after those events Peter would unfortunately also lose Gwen in an ensuing battle that followed sometime later against the Green Goblin, as she died as a result of Parker being unable to save her from falling to her death, this events broke Peter more since despite having cope with Gwen's death he still was never be able to forgive himself for both Gwen and her father's dead, since he was left guilt-driven by those unfortunate events. [[File:Screenshot 2023-04-21 11.01.17 AM.png|thumb|left|250px|Spider-Man falling in love with [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-616)|Mary Jane]]]] A couple of years after that, Parker would fall in love with Mary Jane, managing to enter a relationship with her, who became Peter's confidant. They had a stable relationship to the point Peter would reveal his secret to her, which would make their relationship become stronger. Concurrently Peter, MJ and most of his friends graduated college with Peter gaining a Biophysics degree as Harry became the new heads of Oscorp as he and Liz began dating and got married, a celebration that was witness by Peter and MJ. Spider-Man would also worked with other heroes of his city during this period of time, with the main example being The Hulk, who after a rough start both of them became close friends as they join forces to defeat some threats on many occasions. Apart from the Hulk, Peter also began to team up with other heroes of his time such as the Avengers, with Spider-Man having a kind of a tense relationship with some members such as Tony Stark, who Peter began to gained a rivalry relationship with, as well with Hawkeye. Still, Spider-Man had a few friend who form part of this team apart from Hulk despite his relationship with the rest of its members. Apart from teaming up with other heroes Spider-Man would also defeated other villains apart from the ones of his own rouge gallery such as H.Y.D.R.A.The Venom site on twitter "[https://twitter.com/thevenomsite/status/1338673873093529603?lang=en Storyboards for Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse show that Venom, and other villains that were originally set to appear in Peter B. Parker's origin flashback.]" ====Marriage and Further Crime-Fighting==== {{Biography Quote|I got married.|Spider-Man|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Screenshot 2023-04-21 11.01.21 AM.png|thumb|right|250px|Peter marries with Mary Jane Watson]] Some years later after those events, Peter would marry Mary Jane in a Jewish wedding ceremony, a celebration that was witnessed by everyone important to Peter Parker as well as Mary Jane, J. Jonah Jameson, Flash Thompson and Harry Osborn along with his wife, Liz Allan would get invited to the event along Mary Jane's aunt and uncle. Spider-Man would continued to save the city as well as defeating villains facing old villains as well as new like [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver_Sable Silver Sable] or [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puma_(character) Puma] which he both defeated concurrently. [[File:Comic Scene Into Spider-Verse.png|thumb|left|250px|Spider-Man faces against [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver_Sable Silver Sable] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puma_(character) Puma]]] Following those events, Peter would still go on to fight various villains after his marriage and would go on to save New York City and its inhabitants from the destruction so many times, to the point where he would face so many enemies, and so many weird, dangerous and unusual situations that he began to learn all the tropes of the superhero business. At some point after getting married, Peter bonded with a symbiote which gained him a new black suit which boosted all of his abilities which he began to use as a way to continue being Spider-Man despite the fact that he was was approaching middle age as well as getting a bit tired of the beats he gets in his hero life, something that went away once the symbiote and him fused together. Concurrently Peter also lose another police officer close to him, Jean DeWolff who was killed by an assassin that was ultimately bring down by Spider-Man thing that would also break his hearth. And eventually would quit his job at The Daily Bugle following those events, even though he would end up in good terms with both Jonah and Robbie. [[File:Earth-616B_Storyboard.png|right|thumb|250x250px|Peter's further adventures as Spider-Man following his marriage]] Although Parker initially liked the symbiote, little by little the suit was making him more violent towards criminals to the point of almost leaving them orbundus. After he realizes the effect this suit is having on his personality, Peter managed to discovering that the suit was actually an alien with a mind of its own, which is why Parker got rid of the suit, reverting to the classic red and blue. However, some time after these events, a resentful symbiote returned to haunt him and his loved ones, managing to merge with his old rival of the Bugle: Eddie Brock, with both transforming into one of his most dangerous enemies, Venom. Eventually Parker was able to stun Venom and Brock with powerful sounds. Managing to defeat them, Venom and Spider-Man would have many battles after this with Spider-Man hardly defeating him on each occasion. [[File:Spider-Man getting Praised.webp|left|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Man finally gaining the recognition he deserves]] Sometime after those events Harry Osborn in order to make up to his father father legacy became the second Green Goblin and decided to finish what he started by killing Spider-Man. Eventually, Spider-Man reconciled with Osborn and became on friendly terms with him, who also became aware of Parker's true identity. Tragically, Osborn passed away in Spider-Man's arms as his best friend, greatly breaking his heart as well as MJ's. Thanks to the many people he managed to save, Spider-Man finally started to gained the love and respect of some citizens of New York to the point of some of them actually defending him from the newspaper of the Bugle. "Well, I am tired.|Peter Parker and another [[Earth-1610|dimension's]] [[May Parker (Earth-1610)| May Parker]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}Outside of his superhero life, Peter's life was rough, after losing his [[Ben Parker (Earth-616)|Uncle Ben]] at a young age, Peter began to possesses a strong sense of responsibility, as shown by how he still held guilt over indirectly causing his [[Benjamin Parker (Earth-616)|Uncle]]'s death. The guilt that this event left him on his youth inspired Peter to used his powers to save his city and protect the inhabitants of New York despite the lack of recognition and respect showing him as someone selfless, honorable and humble. However, as the years passed, he began to become more tired and tedious, once he married with [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-616)|Mary Jane]]. Peter began to show a lack of balance, having a kind of mania produced from being Spider-Man to the point where he began to neglect his relationship with his wife as well as his personal life. While Spider-Man saves the city and was sometimes praised by the citizens. Despite continuing to use his powers to help people as Spiderman, he became tired of caring for everybody else just to return to the life of Peter and end with his life screwing up. This has been shown to wear him down exponentially over the years, causing Peter to develop a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicidal_ideation suicide ideation]. Peter also shows that, thanks to the things that happened to him, he has a careless attitude, not caring so much what might happen to him or the city when the villains terrorize it, since according to his words, Peter always wins no matter the situation. Through his love of science, Parker was surprised to discover Miles' extra abilities, as well as his method of camouflage. However, every time Peter makes a mistake he would use them as learning experiences as he becomes more aware of his flaws and more determined to overcome them. In the time he has been active as Spider-Man, Peter has been both witness and participant to countless violent conflicts over the years; he has witnessed hundreds of violent deaths, including those of close friends and loved ones. He has also been personally subjected to severe injury and near-death experiences--and perhaps most alarmingly of all, the bulk of these experiences happened to Peter before the age of twenty. These experiences have left Peter with severe and enduring trauma, and psychologically affect him to this day. Thanks to that Parker at times has shown to have a guilt complex, as seen when he blamed himself for [[George Stacy (Earth-616)|Captain Stacy]] and his Uncle Ben's deaths. Also, Parker's guilt was one of the main reason he decided to distance himself from his friends, allies and fellow superheroes, as well as divorcing Mary Jane, as he felt deep remorse for putting the people he loved in danger and not be managed to save them from their deaths on time.{{Biography Quote|You're chatty|[[Olivia Octavius]] to Spider-Man|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}As Spider-Man, Parker is courageous and capable, being able to come up with clever strategies due to his scientific expertise. Realizing his past mistakes and shortcomings, Peter was prompted to rekindle his relationship with his ex-wife Mary Jane Watson, deciding after his stay with Miles to overcome his fears and instead of fearing of what could happen in the future, concentrated in the present by having a family with MJ and that distancing himself for the ones he loved in order to protect them is never the answer and instead it just make the things worse. Apart from that, Peter has also shown to have a humble and mature personality which he shows when he is the only one who instead of trying to chase Miles tries to reason with the envelope why the [[Canon Events]] are important. Despite this, he and his daughter ultimately defect from the Spider-Society and join Gwen's team to rescue Miles, showing that Peter places those he cares about over the sake of maintaining the status quo for all universes. {{Biography Quote|That's a no, no|Peter Parker to [[Olivia Octavius]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}When he decided to became Spider-Man, Peter decide to created a suit with a mask in order to conceal his true identity of his villains and even his fellows allies in order to protect them from his foes. This was shown when Parker's first instinct when he was sucked into another universe was to take his mask in order to not reveal his face if he arrives to a place where his secret identity could be in risk. Even tough he was in an alternate universe where everyone already knew who he is, Parker still used normal clothes in order to conceal his identity as a civilian. Peter began to also shown his instinct of maintaining his secret identity when he try to avoid Olivia Octavius of unmasking his face even tough she already knew who she is. Even tough, Peter hide his real identity to all of his close allies. Peter eventually managed to shared it to other people such as Mary Jane, however it was also shown that Parker feels more comfortable without his mask when he is alongside his fellow members of the Spider-Society and Spider-People in general, since he feel less nervous about not concealing his face since most of the spider-people are variants of himself. ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Biography Quote|Don't watch the mouth, watch the hands|Peter Parker to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} * '''Spider Physiology''': Having been bitten by a spider that was exposed to great amounts of radiation, Peter Benjamin Parker gained the proportionate physical capabilities of the spider. The [[Radioactive Spider|radioactive spider]] bite triggered numerous body-wide mutagenic changes within Parker, granting him superhuman strength, speed, toughened flesh, numerous arachnid-like abilities such as enhanced senses which allow him to evade several attacks from his foes. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Spider-Man possesses considerable superhuman strength, due to it being the proportional strength of a spider. Peter possesses the proportionate strength of a spider, allowing him to face foes in combat that would otherwise overwhelm him. His physical strength also extends into his legs, enabling him to be able to jump great distances and heights. Although he is relatively untrained, his strength alone is sufficient to lift a bus before it fell onto the civilians. His incredible strength also allowed him to knock out [[Olivia Octavius]] with only a punch, which is something that not even the [[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|alternate]] [[Gwen Stacy]] could do since she had to trow her many consecutive punches and kicks on the latter to beat her up even tough he was on bad shape and tired at the time. Now that Parker was managed to get on shape Peter's strength seems to be more powerful than before becoming one of New York City's strongest heroes alongside the [[Bruce Banner (Earth-616)|Hulk]]. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Spider-Man's body is inhumanly resistant towards impact forces and blunt force trauma, Peter's body is far tougher and denser than a regular human as he could withstand falls from extreme heights, blunt force trauma and superhuman blows with little damage. Peter even survived and recovered from getting his back broken that might otherwise paralyze ordinary humans. **'''Superhuman Speed''': Spider-Man can run and move much faster than an ordinary human and is adept at dodging quickly. Spider-Man was also quick enough to not be catch up by Olivia Octavius whose speed was faster than most human beings. **'''Superhuman Agility''': After Spider-Man gained his powers, he became as agile as a spider, being able to make movements that would be extremely difficult for a normal human with great facility, capable of swinging around on thin spider-webbing and jumping great distances and heights without difficulty. His bones, muscles, and joints have more elastic strength and durability, allowing him to perform gymnastic and contortionist maneuvers without damaging his bone structure. Spider-Man can leap much higher than a normal human or even an Olympic-level gymnast, enabling him the ability to jump and leap to a height of several stories in a single bound, and easily capable of jumping and leaping from one building over a street to the next. This was shows after he does not only defeated daily foes twice of his size thanks to his agility, but his strength alongside his agility show him capable of fighting and defeating [[Earth-1610|another world]]'s [[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|Prowler]] even tough he was tired something even his [[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|perfect deceased counterpart]] couldn't do. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Spider-Man's advanced musculature produces fewer fatigue toxins during physical activity than an ordinary human. This allows him to exert himself physically for much longer periods of time before fatigue begins to impair him. At his peak, Spider-Man can physically exert himself at his peak capacity for several hours before the build-up of fatigue toxins in his blood begins to impair him. Several accounts depict Spider-Man as able to hold his breath for eight minutes or more. **'''Superhuman Metabolism:''' Spider-Man's advanced allows him to be immune to thing such as illness, injury, intoxication. Thanks to that power, Peter need to eat a considerable amounts of food to maintain his energy, this also make him immune to thing such as gaining weight. However, he can momentarily lose the last perk of this ability if he becomes overly stressed or depressed. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Spider-Man's increased metabolism allows him to rapidly heal and regenerate from harm faster and more extensively than normal humans can recover from. He was able to rapidly heal from a black eye from his pursuit with Miles Morales in less than a day as well as his back after falling through the ground. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': On several occasions it has been shown that Spider-Man possesses reflexes far beyond the capabilities of normal humans. In conjunction with his Spider-Sense, Parker is able to easily dodge a full guns that the scientist of alchemax was throwing at him. Spider-Man also possesses a heightened sense of equilibrium which greatly enhances his balance and coordination. **'''Enhanced Senses''': Spider-Man's senses are greatly enhanced, eleving to any human capabilities. Hence, Parker is able to sense potentially dangerous things shortly before they occur. For example, if an object is being thrown at Spider-Man, he will usually be aware of it, even if it is hurled from far away and he is facing the opposite direction. His senses are so developed that he can see things that are beyond the human eyes. ***'''Spider-Sense''': Spider-Man has shown an extrasensory awareness of impending danger, known as his "Spider-Sense". This sixth sense, offers him near complete awareness of his surroundings, and in conjunction with his reflexes, allows him to instinctively dodge or counter nearly all attacks. His brain intakes and responses to stimuli at an accelerated rate, acting as a precognitive ability to sense potential or immediate danger. This awareness thus implies some kind of intelligence, capable of parsing Spider-Man's surroundings, identifying and critically evaluating a potential threat at a subconscious level, thus alerting Spider-Man of dangers he cannot readily notice at first, allowing him to effectively dodge and counter incoming attacks in combat, including projectiles aimed at him even from a blind spot. Provided with his extraordinary speed and wall-crawling, the Spider-Sense is sufficiently well-linked to his superhuman kinesthetics and reflexes, and it permits Spider-Man to evade all manner of spontaneous dangers by an evidently instinctual exercise of some uncanny reflex. It also gives him omnipresent detection to his surroundings, which is how he web swings without looking where he shoots his webs with ease. In addition his spider-sense gives Peter the omnipresent to recognize other individuals with spider-powers, making them have some sort of connection. **'''Wall Crawling''': Spider-Man can adhere to walls, ceilings, and other surfaces in order to scale them, this ability allows him to stick to any surface when willing himself to, as physical contact with said surfaces creates a locking connection necessary to support his body, even when he is inverted above the ground. Alternatively, Parker could make individual portions of his body adhesive, which allowed him to stick his foot onto the side wall to walk into it. This power also overcomes the outer electron shell's normal behavior of mutual repulsion with other outer electron shells and permits the tremendous potential for electron attraction to prevail. The mentally controlled sub-atomic particle responsible for this has yet to be identified. This ability to affect the attraction between surfaces is so far limited to Spider-Man's body (especially concentrated in his hands and feet) and another object, with an upper limit of several tons per finger. *'''Web Generation''': Through the use of his [[Web-Shooters]], Spider-Man is able to project a substance similar to a spider's web. The web's tensile strength and adhesive properties allow him to use the webs for a variety of purposes, from swinging between buildings to restraining his enemies. ===Former Powers=== *'''Physical Augmentation''': Parker's physical prowess was augmented even further by the Venom symbiote, with Venom's already ability to increase it's host user and Peter's already spider-physiology. With this new power, Peter Parker became unstoppable even tough he slowly began to loose himself as well as the control of it's becoming more resentful and easy to anger. Being the reason why Peter would loose that ability after getting rid of the symbiote. *'''Organic Web Generation''': After bonding with the symbiote, Spider-Man gained the ability to generate and project his webbing without the need of his web-shooters. The black silk is released through a white patches in the the back of its hands, he can utilize his organic webbing in any way he can, such as grabbing onto objects or rounding up individuals. Hence, Parker uses quick, powerful attacks and agile movements while firing his webs to immobilize his enemies. *'''Master Marksman''': Spider-Man possesses extremely keen eyesight, and is able to accurately utilize his Web-Shooters to neutralize his targets during his crime-fighting. *'''Master Acrobat''': Due to his superhuman agility, Spider-Man can perform acrobatic and gymnastic feats, such as flips, with little effort. *'''Indomitable Will''': Peter has also shown have an strong sense of will, something that is show how despite the lows points of his life he continued to be a hero, even though he became tired about it. He also shows to be free from evil temptations and always trying to do good things no matter if he is at his lowest point of live since he kept getting back up. ==Equipment == ===Weapons=== *'''[[Web-Shooters]]''': A pair of wrist-mounted mechanical devices used by Spider-Man to project synthetic webbing. Created by Peter Parker himself, the web-shooters help Peter to release a strong, spider silk-like material that they use for web-swinging and binding, among others, completing his spider's ensemble. Its web-shooting mechanism is activated by a button in the middle of their palm, easily accessible when the middle and ring fingers are folded inwards to reach for the button, resulting in Spider-Man's classic hand gesture. The web-shooters allow him to do web-based attacks and swing from building to building. **'''[[Synthetic Webbing]]''': Created also by Peter Parker himself, this web-like organic chemical substance is meant to emulate the silk created by the common spider, while its composition and tensile strength are substantial enough to take the weight of ten men. This synthetic spider silk is used by Parker to help swing around [[Earth-616|New York City]], bind and tie up enemies, steal weapons, and make other useful applications. Spider-Man combines this synthetic web with agile movements to dodge enemy attacks and often targets the enemies' arms and legs to break their balance and immobilize them. The webbing usually dissolves after an hour. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Spider-Man Suit]]''': The Spider-Man Suit is a specialized suit that Peter Parker used while operating on the streets of New York City as the superhero Spider-Man. Peter Parker wears this classic suit to protect his identity from being exposed, so he doesn't endanger his allies and loved ones in general. The suit has a red and blue appearance with black web lines all over it. The mask lenses are white one-way mirror type lenses rimmed with black. His suit also has a small black spider logo on the chest and a large and round red spider logo on the back. Peter Parker has worn this suit for the rest of his twenty-two years in his crime fighting career. Aside from his classic suit, Peter also has multiple copies of his suit, which he uses as spares versions in case one of his suits gets damaged during a conflict or get destroyed. ===Former Equipment=== *'''[[Venom (Earth-616)|Symbiote Suit]]''': Once bonded to the [[Venom (Earth-616)|Venom symbiote]], Parker wore an all-black variation of his red and blue suit which had a large white spider-emblems on its chest and back that Peter Parker used as his Spider-Man's primary suit. He would rejected it when he discovered its true nature as an sentient alien as well as the negative effects it's have on him. *'''[[Peter Parker's Glasses|Glasses]]''': Peter Parker used to wears a pair of horn-rimmed glasses in order to see thanks to his myopia, since his eyesight at the age of fifteen was approximately 20/100: twenty feet away from an object, as he could only see it as clearly as another person standing one hundred feet away. After gaining his powers, Peters eyesight wad dialed to eleven which result on him discarding his glasses shortly after gaining his powers. ==Facilities== *'''[[Parker Residence]]''': The Parker Residence is the home to Peter Parker as well as his wife and daughter. When his parents died at the age of five, Peter moved into his aunt and uncle's house in Forest Hills, Queens, New York. He lived in the Parker Residence since he was young and loved his Aunt May and Uncle Ben, looking up to them as if they were his parents. He live in that house for most of his life, even tough once he graduated high school and enter college he still stay to live at this house since he had some affection for this house. After moving out from this house after his marriage with Mary Jane the couple would still visit May at the house. Following the death of May the house became foreclosure until Peter and Mary Jane fixed they strained relationship with the Parker Residence becoming their permanent house for them and their new born daughter Mayday. *'''[[Midtown High School]]''': Midtown High School is a high school that Peter Parker used to attend as a student. It also had an Academic Football Team that is led by Flash Thompson and a group of cheerleaders with one of them being Liz Allan. Parker took advantage of the school's equipment for his Spider-Man activity, using the chemistry labs to synthesize his web fluid and hiding his suit in his personal locker. Peter and most of his classmates eventually graduated in this high school with Peter being the student with the highest GPA to date. *'''[[Peter Parker and Mary Jane's Apartment]]''': Following his marriage with Mary Jane, Peter moved into this apartment alongside his wife Mary Jane Watson, since both of them were paying the apartment equally. Peter Parker watch a commercial about investments there which would give him the idea of creating a spider-themed restaurant. *'''[[Peter Parker's Apartment]]''': An apartment rented out by Peter Parker, it had acting as his new home while retaining some belongings from the previous Parker Residence and his apartment with Mary Jane. Following his divorced with Mary Jane and the dead of Aunt May, Parker moved into a new apartment building away from everything as a way to stop hurting the people he loved, he would also buy a sowing machine to be able to fix his Spider-Man suit whenever it's get damaged in order to resume his heroics as a solo hero far from everyone who were closed to him. They aged him older than the popular [[w:c:marvel:Peter Parker (Earth-616)|mainstream versions]] and placed him in "a situation that [he] hasn't been in before" — in this case, becoming a somewhat ineffective mentor. And later as a father.[https://www.fandango.com/movie-news/interview-into-the-spider-verse-producers-phil-lord-and-chris-miller-talk-spider-man-stan-lee-and-reinventing-the-comic-book-movie-753510 Interview: 'Into the Spider-Verse' Producers Phil Lord and Chris Miller Talk Spider-Man, Stan Lee and Reinventing the Comic Book Movie] | Fandango **According to [[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]] and some trailers from the aforementioned movie, this version of Peter Parker is the prime/original version of [[Spider-People|Spider-Man]] and [[Peter Parker]] in the [[Spider-Verse Saga|franchise]].[https://screenrant.com/spider-man-old-peter-parker-spiderverse/ Yes, Spider-Verse's Old Peter is The TRUE Spider-Man] | ScreenRant [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c-necp8sRlw Jake Johnson & Shameik Moore Discuss Their Roles In "Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse"] *It's suggested that Peter B. Parker is Jewish, as he married Mary Jane Watson in a Jewish wedding ceremony. *The "B." in Peter B. Parker stands for the character's usual middle name across his different versions, Benjamin. For Into the Spider-Verse's producer and screenwriter Phil Lord, it also stood for "B grade or B-List or B-picture," to accentuate that Peter B. Parker was a less competent Spider-Man than the one from Miles' universe. He also stated that the meaning of the B can be up to interpretation.Lord, Phil (4 January 2019) Phil Lord on Twitter: [https://twitter.com/philiplord/status/1081314696571940864 "The B stands for Benjamin, per canon. and it can ALSO stand for Burrito. And for me it stood for the B grade or B-List or B-picture. A lesser Peter. You decide what it means for you."] Twitter. Retrieved on 13 January 2020. *While he is based in name on the Peter Parker of Earth 616A (the Marvel Universe) from the comics, and had a pretty similar life to the latter managing to live trough the same events he did on the comics. The differences started to occur when in the comics' Earth 616 Peter never lose his Aunt May and instead make a deal with Mephisto to avoid her from dying, he also is way younger. With the two of them having an age gasp of ten years, however some comics make his age past from 28 to 26. And lately in the new Amazing Spider-Man run, Peter it seems to be now in his early 30s. There is also the fact that comics' Earth 616 Peter never had a baby, even tough he was supposed to have two when he was marriage with Mary Jane, but one died at birth and the other ceased to exist thanks to Mephisto. Another difference is that the comics' Earth 616 Peter arrive to the Ultimate Universe and meet Miles Morales on his own without the assistance of other spider-people the comics Peter also currently shares universe with Miles following the second Secret Wars. This Peter also had more experience with the Multiverse prior meeting Miles since he meet Miguel O'Hara as well as Max Borne earlier on his life. **However a deleted scene of the film shown Peter B. Parker explaining the multiverse to Miles hinting that he already had experience with the subject.
